Job Position:&nbsp;Cashier (Females)

Job Location:&nbsp;Oshodi, Lagos

Responsibilities


	Processing payments accurately
	Issuing Receipts and providing friendly services
	Cash Management
	Customer Services
	Sales Promotion.


Requirements


	The working time and days are from 8am to 6pm and from Mondays to Saturdays and Public Holidays.
	Interested applicants must have a Degree in Accounting and must be residing withi Oshodi axis, Mushin axis and other nearby surrounding axis.
	Applicants must have working experience as a cashier for within 3-5 years in a ware house setting and must be knowledgeable in business and must be computer literate.
	This application is open to female cashiers only.


Salary
N75,000 - N80,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;patricia_asemota@yahoo.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Cashier (Females) Job at Macden Communications Limited




        
    

        
        Macden Communications Limited is into the sale, supply and distribution of beverages and is a major distributor for Internationl Breweries PLC, Nigerian Breweries Limited, Guiness Nigerian Limited.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Cashier (Females)

Job Location: Oshodi, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Processing payments accurately
	Issuing Receipts and providing friendly services
	Cash Management
	Customer Services
	Sales Promotion.


Requirements
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The working time and days are from 8am to 6pm and from Mondays to Saturdays and Public Holidays.
	Interested applicants must have a Degree in Accounting and must be residing withi Oshodi axis, Mushin axis and other nearby surrounding axis.
	Applicants must have working experience as a cashier for within 3-5 years in a ware house setting and must be knowledgeable in business and must be computer literate.
	This application is open to female cashiers only.


Salary
N75,000 - N80,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note: For more inquiries, candidates should call: 09063461304.    

    

        Click here to Apply

title: Job Vacancies at Neveah Limited
content:
Neveah Limited is a leading commodities trading company essentially dealing in the export of solid minerals and agricultural products.

The company was established in 2014 with five operational offices in different states and her head office in Abuja, Nigeria. Our services are centered around sourcing the finest, solid minerals and the healthiest agricultural products from the local market and exporting them to meet the demands of our customers at international markets.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Accountant

Job Location: Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time

Brief Job Overview
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are looking for a Accountant to join our team, a mid-level experienced candidate who will support the Finance/Accounts Unit with the task of guiding businesses and individuals in decisions about spending money to attain profit.
	You will analyse data, evaluate investment opportunities, and provide recommendations to stakeholders/Firms.


Responsibilities
The ideal candidate will (but not limited to):


	Ensures general ledger, subsidiary ledgers, and other financial records and schedules are accurately maintained.
	Institutes policies and procedures capable of protecting the business resources against waste, fraud, and inefficiency.
	Maintains procedures to establish tight control over cash transactions.
	Ensures timely follow up of accounts receivable and payable.
	Evaluates & implements internal and external audit recommendations & provides feedback to management on results.
	Implements budget and forecast preparation schedule by collecting relevant business data and projections from the line departments and provide relevant analyses and feedback.
	Provides support, service and advice in all finance matters to the company’s management.
	Exercises budgetary controls, accounting & physical controls and credit controls as per laid down procedures and asset management.
	Maintains an effective involvement and understanding of the business to contribute to key business decisions within the Company through financial advice and expertise.
	Provide creative alternatives and recommendations to reduce costs and improve financial performance.
	Identify trends, advise the company and recommend actions to a senior financial analyst based on sound analysis
	Gain and update job knowledge to remain informed about novelty in the field
	Consult with management to guide and influence long-term and strategic decision-making within the broadest scope
	Drive process improvement and policy development initiatives that impact the function
	Other duties as may be assigned.


Qualifications


	B.Sc Degree in Finance, Economics or a related field
	A qualified chartered accountant with 3 - 5 years of experience in a similar role within the manufacturing/export Industry
	Proven working experience as a Finance Analyst
	Proficient in spreadsheets, databases, MS Office and financial software applications
	Hands-on experience with statistical analysis and statistical packages
	Outstanding presentation, reporting and communication skills
	Proven knowledge of financial forecasting and diagnosis, corporate finance and information analysis
	Well informed in current financial subjects, accounting, tax laws, money market and business environments
	ICAN/ ACCA Professional Membership is required.
	Must be able to use modern accounting software.


Required Competencies:


	Technical Accounting Skills.
	Mastery of Accounting Principles
	Proven work experience as a Finance Analyst or similar role
	Solid knowledge of relevant regulatory standards
	Good communication skills, both verbal and written
	Excellent data collection and analysis skills
	Strong attention to detail
	Relevant training and certifications.
	Multi-tasking skills, with the ability to prioritize tasks.


Salary
N350,000 - N400,000 per month  

Application Closing Date: 25th March, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

 

 

2.) Internal Audit Officer

Job Location: Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Overview


	TheInternal Audit Officer will support the Internal Audit Department in reviewing financial, operational, and compliance processes to ensure adherence to company policies, internal controls, and statutory requirements.
	The role involves conducting audits, risk assessments, and control evaluations across business functions while contributing to continuous improvement and organizational efficiency.


Key Responsibilities


	Review and evaluate financial, operational, and compliance processes to ensure adherence to company policies and statutory regulations.
	Conduct risk assessments to identify internal control weaknesses and propose remedial actions.
	Perform internal audits and special investigations to ensure accuracy, transparency, and proper audit trails.
	Review accounts receivable, accounts payable, and ledger postings to ensure accurate financial records.
	Conduct inventory and cash count verifications to detect potential fraud, wastage, or discrepancies.
	Verify that procurement, logistics, and stock management processes comply with company policies and cost-control measures.
	Review adherence to Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) and recommend process improvements.
	Prepare comprehensive audit reports highlighting findings, risk ratings, and recommendations.
	Present audit outcomes to management and follow up on agreed corrective action plans.


Smart KPIs


	Audit Report Timeliness:Submit audit reports within 3 working days after review completion.
	Control Testing Accuracy:Achieve ≥ 95% accuracy in control testing and documentation.
	Audit Findings Closure Rate:Ensure ≥ 90% of audit findings are closed within agreed timelines.
	Compliance Adherence:Maintain ≥ 98% compliance with audit standards and company procedures.
	Risk Mitigation Effectiveness:Demonstrate measurable improvement in identified risk areas within 3 months post-audit.


Qualifications & Experience


	B.Sc Degree / HND in Accounting, Finance, or a related field.
	2 - 4 years of relevant experience in auditing, accounting, or internal control roles.
	Membership in ICAN, CISA, CFE, or CIA is an added advantage.
	Experience in amanufacturing environmentis preferred.


Skills & Competencies:


	Solid understanding of accounting principles and internal auditing standards.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and ERP/accounting software.
	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
	Excellent report writing and documentation ability.
	High level of integrity, accuracy, and attention to detail.
	Good communication and interpersonal skills.
	Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines.


Application Closing Date: 25th March, 2026.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should submit their CV and cover letter to: [email protected] using" Internal Audit Officer – Mowe" as the subject of the mail.

Note: Only properly submitted applications and qualified candidates will be considered.

 

3.) Planning & Warehouse Operations Manager

Job Location: Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Overview


	Oversee Sales & Operations Planning (S&OP), end-to-end warehouse operations, and ensure timely material flows.


Key Responsibilities


	Lead S&OP cycles; align demand forecasts with production capacity.
	Manage receiving, put?away, picking, packing, and shipping processes.
	Ensure accurate scrap analysis per lot and timely reporting.
	Optimize dock?to?stock turnaround time and storage utilization.
	Drive continuous improvement in warehouse layout and workflows.


SMART KPIs


	Receiving Accuracy: ≥99.5% accuracy rate monthly.
	Dock-to-Stock TAT: Average ≤4 hours for all inbound shipments.
	Scrap Reporting: 100% lot scrap analysis reports delivered within 24 hours post-production.
	Warehouse Utilization: Achieve ≥85% space utilization without congestion.


Qualifications & Experience


	Bachelor’s in Supply Chain, Logistics, or related field.
	6+ years’ experience, including 3 in manufacturing S&OP and warehouse.


Skills & Competencies:


	Expertise in S&OP and WMS/ERP systems.
	Leadership and cross?functional collaboration.


Application Closing Date: 22nd March, 2026.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their updated CV saved in their full name to: [email protected] using the Job Posiiton  as the subject of the email.

Note: Only properly submitted applications and qualified candidates will be considered.

     

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "HR Operations & Reward Manager",
  "description": "PZ Cussons is a dynamic consumer products group and innovator of some of the world&#39;s best known and loved brands. Calling on over 130 years of expertise, we operate internationally in carefully selected developed and emerging markets that present the greatest strategic potential for future growth. Our world-class supply chain and distribution networks enable us to meet our global consumer needs and deliver quality brands that add value and enhance everyday lives.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;HR Operations &amp; Reward Manager&nbsp;

Job ID:&nbsp;JR002770
Job Location:&nbsp;Ilupeju, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities
Rewards Management:


	Drive the development and implementation of compensation and benefits frameworks, salary reviews, and incentive schemes
	Manage HR shared services operations including employee data, HRIS systems, and service-level agreements to ensure smooth delivery of HR services
	Ensure adherence to labor laws, internal policies, and audit requirements
	Drive automation, digital transformation, and continuous improvement in HR processes
	Provide guidance on reward policies and HR services, acting as a point of escalation for complex queries
	Collaborate with HR Business Partners, Finance, and external vendors to align reward and shared services with organizational goals
	Support the design and implementation of Africa-wide reward strategies aligned with global philosophy, local regulations, and business priorities
	Provide input into HR aspects of mergers and acquisitions due diligence, integration planning, and harmonization of pay and benefits
	Support salary reviews, job grading, benchmarking, and incentive schemes
	Prepare regular reward and workforce cost reports for senior leadership
	Review and update policies in line with changing legislation and market practices


Payroll and HR Compliance:


	Oversee accurate payroll processing, benefits enrollment, and compliance with statutory requirements including tax, labor, and social security laws
	Oversee employee data management while ensuring HRIS integrity and accurate records
	Ensure compliance with data protection legislation such as GDPR, NDPR, and local equivalents
	Ensure all HR compliance audits, both statutory and internal, are effectively managed
	Conduct monthly payroll reconciliations and compliance checks
	Submit mandatory reports to government agencies and internal audit or controls teams (ICFR)
	Conduct periodic reviews of employee data across records and systems to ensure accuracy, integrity, and completeness


Employee Welfare and Medicals:


	Supervise medical services, canteen or catering services, employee product vouchers, site clinics and ambulance services, long service awards, and other employee welfare initiatives
	Develop and oversee welfare and employee wellbeing strategies that support engagement, productivity, and retention
	Design medical and wellness policies aligned with business needs and local regulations
	Oversee administration of medical insurance schemes and wellness initiatives
	Act as an escalation point for employee welfare issues
	Ensure cost-effective vendor management and service delivery for welfare and medical programs
	Review and renegotiate contracts with welfare service providers
	Track welfare activities and successes, including documentation for quarterly board reports


Education and Certifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree or certifications in a relevant field
	Master&rsquo;s degree
	Relevant HR certifications such as CIPD, SHRM, HRCI, or equivalent


Experience:


	5&ndash;8 years of progressive HR experience including at least 3&ndash;4 years in reward, compensation and benefits, or shared services roles
	Experience working across multiple markets will be an added advantage
	Proven experience in payroll management, HR compliance, HRIS, and employee records
	Strong exposure to industrial relations and unionised environments
	Experience supporting HR due diligence and integration in mergers and acquisitions is a strong advantage


Skills and Competencies:


	Exceptional stakeholder management and influencing skills
	Ability to remain productive and professional in a high-pressure work environment
	Deep understanding of reward frameworks, HR systems, and compliance requirements across Africa
	Knowledge of data protection laws and their application in HR
	Strong analytical, problem-solving, and decision-making skills
	High integrity, resilience, and ability to manage sensitive issues with discretion
	Excellent knowledge of Microsoft Office, especially Excel, PowerPoint, and Word
	Ability to provide clear and accurate information in a suitable format for business use
	Experience in data analysis, data manipulation, and problem-solving
	Confidence and skill when presenting information to different audiences

HR Operations & Reward Manager Job at PZ Cussons




        
    

        
        PZ Cussons is a dynamic consumer products group and innovator of some of the world's best known and loved brands. Calling on over 130 years of expertise, we operate internationally in carefully selected developed and emerging markets that present the greatest strategic potential for future growth. Our world-class supply chain and distribution networks enable us to meet our global consumer needs and deliver quality brands that add value and enhance everyday lives.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HR Operations & Reward Manager 

Job ID: JR002770
Job Location: Ilupeju, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Responsibilities
Rewards Management:


	Drive the development and implementation of compensation and benefits frameworks, salary reviews, and incentive schemes
	Manage HR shared services operations including employee data, HRIS systems, and service-level agreements to ensure smooth delivery of HR services
	Ensure adherence to labor laws, internal policies, and audit requirements
	Drive automation, digital transformation, and continuous improvement in HR processes
	Provide guidance on reward policies and HR services, acting as a point of escalation for complex queries
	Collaborate with HR Business Partners, Finance, and external vendors to align reward and shared services with organizational goals
	Support the design and implementation of Africa-wide reward strategies aligned with global philosophy, local regulations, and business priorities
	Provide input into HR aspects of mergers and acquisitions due diligence, integration planning, and harmonization of pay and benefits
	Support salary reviews, job grading, benchmarking, and incentive schemes
	Prepare regular reward and workforce cost reports for senior leadership
	Review and update policies in line with changing legislation and market practices


Payroll and HR Compliance:


	Oversee accurate payroll processing, benefits enrollment, and compliance with statutory requirements including tax, labor, and social security laws
	Oversee employee data management while ensuring HRIS integrity and accurate records
	Ensure compliance with data protection legislation such as GDPR, NDPR, and local equivalents
	Ensure all HR compliance audits, both statutory and internal, are effectively managed
	Conduct monthly payroll reconciliations and compliance checks
	Submit mandatory reports to government agencies and internal audit or controls teams (ICFR)
	Conduct periodic reviews of employee data across records and systems to ensure accuracy, integrity, and completeness


Employee Welfare and Medicals:


	Supervise medical services, canteen or catering services, employee product vouchers, site clinics and ambulance services, long service awards, and other employee welfare initiatives
	Develop and oversee welfare and employee wellbeing strategies that support engagement, productivity, and retention
	Design medical and wellness policies aligned with business needs and local regulations
	Oversee administration of medical insurance schemes and wellness initiatives
	Act as an escalation point for employee welfare issues
	Ensure cost-effective vendor management and service delivery for welfare and medical programs
	Review and renegotiate contracts with welfare service providers
	Track welfare activities and successes, including documentation for quarterly board reports


Education and Certifications


	Bachelor’s degree or certifications in a relevant field
	Master’s degree
	Relevant HR certifications such as CIPD, SHRM, HRCI, or equivalent


Experience:


	5–8 years of progressive HR experience including at least 3–4 years in reward, compensation and benefits, or shared services roles
	Experience working across multiple markets will be an added advantage
	Proven experience in payroll management, HR compliance, HRIS, and employee records
	Strong exposure to industrial relations and unionised environments
	Experience supporting HR due diligence and integration in mergers and acquisitions is a strong advantage


Skills and Competencies:


	Exceptional stakeholder management and influencing skills
	Ability to remain productive and professional in a high-pressure work environment
	Deep understanding of reward frameworks, HR systems, and compliance requirements across Africa
	Knowledge of data protection laws and their application in HR
	Strong analytical, problem-solving, and decision-making skills
	High integrity, resilience, and ability to manage sensitive issues with discretion
	Excellent knowledge of Microsoft Office, especially Excel, PowerPoint, and Word
	Ability to provide clear and accurate information in a suitable format for business use
	Experience in data analysis, data manipulation, and problem-solving
	Confidence and skill when presenting information to different audiences
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Director of Generative AI Operations",
  "description": "Hugo is a Future-of-Work company. We build and manage remote teams in black communities for some of the world&rsquo;s largest technology and media companies. We specialize in end-to-end machine learning data annotation and omnichannel support solutions that ultimately yield more inclusive outcomes for both black communities globally and our clients.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Director of Generative AI Operations

Job Location:&nbsp;Nigeria Office (Remote)
Department:&nbsp;Service Delivery, AI Operations

What You&#39;ll Be Doing


	The Director of Generative AI Operations, based remotely with West Africa and US time zone coverage, leads the end-to-end delivery, scaling, and evolution of Hugo&rsquo;s Generative AI and data annotation services for global enterprise clients.
	The role reports to the VP of Global Generative AI Operations and owns operational excellence across complex AI workflows, client-facing delivery strategy, workforce systems, quality frameworks, and pilot-to-scale execution.
	The role sits at the intersection of AI product understanding, distributed workforce orchestration, and enterprise client partnership.
	The mandate centers on building and scaling operating models for hyper-agile AI delivery, where requirements evolve quickly, and solutions are developed rapidly alongside execution, while protecting quality, speed, client trust, and team wellbeing as demand changes.


Key Responsibilities
Generative AI Delivery Leadership:


	Own delivery across multiple GenAI and advanced ML workflows, including pilots, scaled programs, and experimental work
	Lead AI Ops programs that leverage distributed, crowd-based, or hybrid workforce models
	Translate client objectives into executable operating models, staffing strategies, and metrics
	Ensure consistent delivery outcomes across quality, AHT, throughput, and SLA adherence
	Partner with Quality and Training teams to embed Day-1 excellence and continuous improvement


Pilot Design and Scale-Up Strategy:


	Lead Hugo&rsquo;s pilots&#39; operating model, including intake, scoping, staffing, execution, and ROI
	Design fast, low-risk pilots that validate quality, rater profiles, tooling fit, and workflow complexity
	Operate comfortably in hyper-agile environments where guidelines, evaluation criteria, and tooling evolve in parallel with delivery
	Identify repeatable patterns that convert pilots into long-term revenue-generating programs
	Maintain visibility into headcount deployment, cost efficiency, and capability progression


Workforce and Capability Strategy:


	Design and operate flexible AI workforce models spanning on-payroll teams, crowd contributors, and specialized talent pools
	Oversee certification pathways, performance calibration, and specialization tracks
	Partner with training and quality teams to evolve skill standards as AI task complexity increases
	Balance utilization, growth, and contributor wellbeing across a fast-changing demand environment


Client Partnership and Value Creation:


	Act as a senior delivery and ops counterpart to client PjMs, product leaders, and research teams
	Lead structured client conversations on delivery performance, system design tradeoffs, and roadmap planning
	Proactively surface insights on workflow optimization, capability expansion, and operational levers
	Support commercial discussions by grounding proposals in delivery feasibility, speed, and data


Metrics, Systems, and Operational Rigor:


	Define and own performance dashboards across quality, efficiency, workforce health, and finances
	Build operational systems and feedback loops that scale beyond individual programs or clients
	Establish SOPs, escalation paths, and governance frameworks suited to fast-moving AI work
	Drive post-mortems, root-cause analyses, and continuous improvement cycles


Leadership and Org Development:


	Lead and develop senior portfolio managers, project managers, and cross-functional partners
	Shape org design and capability investments as Hugo scales its AI operations footprint
	Serve as a culture carrier for execution excellence, clarity, and accountability
	Mentor emerging leaders and build succession pathways across the delivery organization


If you are excited about delivering the following outcomes, then this role is for you:


	High-quality, scalable GenAI delivery across pilots and mature programs
	Faster and more confident conversion from experimental work to sustained client engagements
	Clear operating models for crowd-based and distributed AI delivery
	Improved unit economics without compromising quality or workforce health
	Strong client confidence in Hugo&rsquo;s ability to handle complex, evolving AI work


What Qualification You&#39;ll Need
Required Experience and Skills:


	8&ndash;12+ years in operations, program leadership, or delivery roles, with progressive responsibility leading AI, ML, or data-intensive programs in recent years
	Significant experience running AI development or AI operations programs that leverage crowd, freelance, or distributed workforce models
	Demonstrated success operating in hyper-agile environments with evolving requirements
	Experience leading distributed, multicultural teams
	Proven ability to translate ambiguity into scalable delivery systems
	Strong analytical orientation with comfort using metrics to guide decisions
	Executive-level communication and client-facing judgment


Strongly Preferred:


	Delivery leadership experience (PM level or higher) at crowd-focused AI or distributed delivery companies, such as Mercor, Invisible, Turing, Snorkel, Scale AI, Surge, or other similar crowd-based AI delivery organizations
	Familiarity with ML annotation, RLHF-style workflows, or human-in-the-loop AI systems


Leadership Traits:


	High ownership with a strong bias toward execution
	Comfort operating in ambiguity and rapid change
	Systems thinker who balances speed with rigor
	Calm, credible presence in high-stakes, diverse client conversations
	Strong people leader with a focus on growth, sustainability, and accountability


What We Provide


	Hugo offers a hybrid work environment that balances employee flexibility with a collegial, fun office culture. We pride ourselves on offering a dynamic environment where ambitious professionals can make a measurable impact and accelerate their careers. Our compensation and benefits are highly competitive.
",

Director of Generative AI Operations Job at Hugo




        
    

        
        Hugo is a Future-of-Work company. We build and manage remote teams in black communities for some of the world’s largest technology and media companies. We specialize in end-to-end machine learning data annotation and omnichannel support solutions that ultimately yield more inclusive outcomes for both black communities globally and our clients.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Director of Generative AI Operations

Job Location: Nigeria Office (Remote)
Department: Service Delivery, AI Operations

What You'll Be Doing
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Director of Generative AI Operations, based remotely with West Africa and US time zone coverage, leads the end-to-end delivery, scaling, and evolution of Hugo’s Generative AI and data annotation services for global enterprise clients.
	The role reports to the VP of Global Generative AI Operations and owns operational excellence across complex AI workflows, client-facing delivery strategy, workforce systems, quality frameworks, and pilot-to-scale execution.
	The role sits at the intersection of AI product understanding, distributed workforce orchestration, and enterprise client partnership.
	The mandate centers on building and scaling operating models for hyper-agile AI delivery, where requirements evolve quickly, and solutions are developed rapidly alongside execution, while protecting quality, speed, client trust, and team wellbeing as demand changes.


Key Responsibilities
Generative AI Delivery Leadership:


	Own delivery across multiple GenAI and advanced ML workflows, including pilots, scaled programs, and experimental work
	Lead AI Ops programs that leverage distributed, crowd-based, or hybrid workforce models
	Translate client objectives into executable operating models, staffing strategies, and metrics
	Ensure consistent delivery outcomes across quality, AHT, throughput, and SLA adherence
	Partner with Quality and Training teams to embed Day-1 excellence and continuous improvement


Pilot Design and Scale-Up Strategy:


	Lead Hugo’s pilots' operating model, including intake, scoping, staffing, execution, and ROI
	Design fast, low-risk pilots that validate quality, rater profiles, tooling fit, and workflow complexity
	Operate comfortably in hyper-agile environments where guidelines, evaluation criteria, and tooling evolve in parallel with delivery
	Identify repeatable patterns that convert pilots into long-term revenue-generating programs
	Maintain visibility into headcount deployment, cost efficiency, and capability progression


Workforce and Capability Strategy:


	Design and operate flexible AI workforce models spanning on-payroll teams, crowd contributors, and specialized talent pools
	Oversee certification pathways, performance calibration, and specialization tracks
	Partner with training and quality teams to evolve skill standards as AI task complexity increases
	Balance utilization, growth, and contributor wellbeing across a fast-changing demand environment


Client Partnership and Value Creation:


	Act as a senior delivery and ops counterpart to client PjMs, product leaders, and research teams
	Lead structured client conversations on delivery performance, system design tradeoffs, and roadmap planning
	Proactively surface insights on workflow optimization, capability expansion, and operational levers
	Support commercial discussions by grounding proposals in delivery feasibility, speed, and data


Metrics, Systems, and Operational Rigor:


	Define and own performance dashboards across quality, efficiency, workforce health, and finances
	Build operational systems and feedback loops that scale beyond individual programs or clients
	Establish SOPs, escalation paths, and governance frameworks suited to fast-moving AI work
	Drive post-mortems, root-cause analyses, and continuous improvement cycles


Leadership and Org Development:


	Lead and develop senior portfolio managers, project managers, and cross-functional partners
	Shape org design and capability investments as Hugo scales its AI operations footprint
	Serve as a culture carrier for execution excellence, clarity, and accountability
	Mentor emerging leaders and build succession pathways across the delivery organization


If you are excited about delivering the following outcomes, then this role is for you:


	High-quality, scalable GenAI delivery across pilots and mature programs
	Faster and more confident conversion from experimental work to sustained client engagements
	Clear operating models for crowd-based and distributed AI delivery
	Improved unit economics without compromising quality or workforce health
	Strong client confidence in Hugo’s ability to handle complex, evolving AI work


What Qualification You'll Need
Required Experience and Skills:


	8–12+ years in operations, program leadership, or delivery roles, with progressive responsibility leading AI, ML, or data-intensive programs in recent years
	Significant experience running AI development or AI operations programs that leverage crowd, freelance, or distributed workforce models
	Demonstrated success operating in hyper-agile environments with evolving requirements
	Experience leading distributed, multicultural teams
	Proven ability to translate ambiguity into scalable delivery systems
	Strong analytical orientation with comfort using metrics to guide decisions
	Executive-level communication and client-facing judgment


Strongly Preferred:


	Delivery leadership experience (PM level or higher) at crowd-focused AI or distributed delivery companies, such as Mercor, Invisible, Turing, Snorkel, Scale AI, Surge, or other similar crowd-based AI delivery organizations
	Familiarity with ML annotation, RLHF-style workflows, or human-in-the-loop AI systems


Leadership Traits:


	High ownership with a strong bias toward execution
	Comfort operating in ambiguity and rapid change
	Systems thinker who balances speed with rigor
	Calm, credible presence in high-stakes, diverse client conversations
	Strong people leader with a focus on growth, sustainability, and accountability


What We Provide


	Hugo offers a hybrid work environment that balances employee flexibility with a collegial, fun office culture. We pride ourselves on offering a dynamic environment where ambitious professionals can make a measurable impact and accelerate their careers. Our compensation and benefits are highly competitive.
